expectations.text: References or applies the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle (or test-driven development) for skill authoring
expectations.evidence: No mention of RED-GREEN-REFACTOR, TDD, or test-driven development anywhere in the output.

expectations.text: Mentions pressure scenarios, failure modes, or edge cases as the starting point for skill design
expectations.evidence: Lists missing content like group dynamics and edge cases, but frames them as gaps in the skill's guidance, not as a starting-point methodology for skill design.

expectations.text: Flags that the skill lacks failure modes, pressure scenarios, or edge cases
expectations.evidence: Point 7: 'No handling of group dynamics... what to do when one person dominates, when conflict arises, when energy drops, or when the group gets stuck.'

expectations.text: Points out that there are no test cases or verification criteria to know if the skill actually works
expectations.evidence: Never mentions testing the skill against scenarios or any verification criteria. Critique focuses on content gaps, not on the absence of a test/validation process.
